findings.evidence: line 75 proof ⟨continuous_charFun, charFun_isPositiveDefinite_of_star_eq_neg hstar⟩ vs line 89 ⟨continuous_charFun, charFun_isPositiveDefinite_of_star_eq_neg hstar, charFun_star_kernel_...⟩
findings.fix: Drop the pair lemma and keep only the triple (callers take .1/.2.1), or drop both bundles since the standalone lemmas already exist.
findings.issue: continuous_charFun_and_isPositiveDefinite_of_star_eq_neg is subsumed by continuous_charFun_and_isPositiveDefinite_and_kernel_of_star_eq_neg (the triple) — its statement is just the triple's first two conjuncts, with an identical proof. Both are trivial conjunctions of the three standalone lemmas already in the file.

findings.evidence: line 47 kernel (a-b) and line 56 kernel (a+star b) both prove via `simpa ... using charFun_posSemidef (fun x => x)`; hstar makes a+star b = a-b
findings.fix: Derive it from charFun_isPositiveDefiniteKernel by rewriting the function with hstar/sub_eq_add_neg instead of re-deriving from charFun_posSemidef.
findings.issue: charFun_star_kernel_isPositiveDefiniteKernel_of_star_eq_neg re-proves charFun_isPositiveDefiniteKernel from charFun_posSemidef; under hstar the kernel fun a b => charFun μ (a + star b) is the same as fun a b => charFun μ (a - b).

summary: The core bridge to IsPositiveDefinite is new and not duplicative, but the file ships two redundant package lemmas: the continuity+PD pair is a strict projection of the continuity+PD+kernel triple (same hypotheses and proof). The two kernel lemmas are also the same kernel under the star=-negation hypothesis.
